stepsShipped.deliverable: Restores the Cluster A multi-company UI foundation per audit rows 2.2 / 2.3 / 2.4 (foundation for 2.2 WorkspaceSwitcher today; 2.3 CreateCompanyDialog lands in step b; 2.4 CompanySettings in step c). Ships the top-bar workspace switcher + its event-sync plumbing atomically. The switcher renders immediately on app boot with a loading Skeleton in the trigger, swaps to the active company name once useCompanies resolves, and lists every company in the dropdown for click-to-switch. Cross-process company lifecycle events (company.created emitted by M-C step b, company.updated by M-C step e, company.archived pre-M-C, company.deleted by M-C step e) invalidate the ['companies'] React Query cache via the new global-scope useCompanyEventSync — so an E2E spec, a command-palette flow, or an agent-initiated archive leaves the switcher self-consistent with zero manual refetch. The step (b) 'Create company…' CTA ships as a disabled placeholder labelled 'Soon' so the menu stays honest about current capability (no dead-click race against an unwired IPC). The plan doc lands in the same atomic per M-E S1.

designDecisions.globalScopeEventSync: useCompanyEventSync is GLOBAL-SCOPE — no companyId argument, no event.companyId guard, effect deps [qc] only. Per-company hooks use [companyId, qc] with event.companyId !== companyId returning early. The switcher lives ABOVE the active-company selection in the top-bar and must invalidate on every company lifecycle event regardless of which company is active — a copy-paste of the per-company guard would silently drop company.created events for brand-new companies whose id never matches the current active companyId. The cross-hook contract iterator in event-sync-hooks.test.ts is split accordingly: the mount-once [companyId, qc] pattern iteration EXCLUDES use-companies (inline architectural note), and a dedicated describe block pins the global-scope alternate contract.
designDecisions.switcherMountPoint: Top-bar between Strategia brand block and nav tabs, separated by vertical Separators on both sides. Sidenav is reserved for view navigation; user-menu pattern isn't yet established. Top-bar gives the switcher status-bar gravity (like GitHub's repo picker) without adding a new app-shell section. Single mount guarantees useCompanyEventSync cannot unmount mid-session — a per-view mount would drop the subscription when the user switches away from that view.
designDecisions.stepBPlaceholder: Create company CTA renders DISABLED with 'Soon' badge + title='Available in step (b)' until step b ships CreateCompanyDialog. Rationale: a visible-but-dead button would race a mid-session click against an unwired IPC, leaving the user in a half-state. The DropdownMenuItem's data-[disabled] styling + Radix's pointer-events-none guard make the click physically impossible while the label stays honest about the roadmap. Anchor: data-workspace-switcher-action='create-company-placeholder' so the step (b) ship can flip it live with a one-line change.
designDecisions.companyWireShapeStatus: Company interface in shared-types does NOT carry archive status today — companies.list returns every row. Switcher renders ALL rows for the step (a) ship. Step (c) CompanySettings will widen the Company interface alongside the archived-companies surfacing UX, at which point the switcher filters to status === 'active'. In the dev default (single company, never archived) this has zero visible effect. Documented as a TODO inline in use-companies.ts.
designDecisions.stableDataSelectors: Every 5 F10 state + every company row + the trigger + the content panel + the create-company placeholder CTA carry a stable data-workspace-switcher-* attribute for the step (g) E2E. Mirrors the M34 T7 data-copilot-toolbar-toggle precedent + the step-card-kind data-step-kind convention. Keeps the E2E specs robust against CSS-class + JSX-structure drift.
designDecisions.sourceStringAuditTest: workspace-switcher.test.tsx uses pure source-string readFileSync + regex assertions rather than jsdom + RTL. Matches the repo-wide convention (every renderer test under environment: 'node' per apps/desktop/vitest.config.ts — see top-bar.test.tsx, audit-event-chip.test.tsx, event-sync-hooks.test.ts). Faster, no jsdom overhead, catches the regressions that matter (mount drops, state markers missing, ARIA lost, data-* anchors renamed) first and cheapest. Visual + interactive behavior is covered by the step (g) E2E spec.

S2 conformance gate expected GREEN on 5d0c5bc — no new IPC channels + no allowlist movement (preserved at 92/3/0). S3 pre-commit hook clean on staged diff — no CLAUDE.md edits in this atomic (M-D status is tracked on this ledger, not on the CLAUDE.md status blocks; M-F rewrites those per the three-bucket convention). S4 verifiedBy populated on this paired ledger entry per contract.

NEW features/workspace/create-company-dialog.tsx dialog form (name / slug / settings stub / icon / theme) wired to ipc.companies.create with client-side validation mirroring the M-C step b handler contract (/^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]{0,62}$/ slug regex + name trim ≤120 chars + auto-suggest slug from name until user manually edits). Swaps the disabled 'Soon' placeholder in the WorkspaceSwitcher for a live 'Create company…' CTA that opens the dialog. Success → setCompanyId(result.companyId) + close + toast; error → inline field errors + keep dialog open. Companion test file workspace-switcher update narrowing the placeholder assertion. No new IPC channels (companies.create already shipped in M-C step b). Allowlist preserved at 3.

stepsShipped.deliverable: Closes the 2026-04-19 ground-zero audit's 1 P1 + 2 of 3 P2 findings inline + collapses step (b) CreateCompanyDialog forward into the same atomic per Rocky's iron-rule directive against deferred placeholders. The placeholder 'Soon' Create CTA is REJECTED — the switcher now opens a fully-wired CreateCompanyDialog, and the M-D Cluster A multi-company create flow is end-to-end usable today. The P1.1 stale-active-companyId guard fix replaces the App.tsx one-shot preload-bridge fetch with the reactive useCompanies hook, widening the auto-select condition from 'companyId === null' to 'companyId === null OR active company is no longer in the list' — closing the silent-failure path where a user who deleted their active workspace was left with every downstream view pointing at a dead row. The P2.1 error-row refactor swaps a dead-end 'retry in a moment' message for an actionable Retry item that calls refetch directly. The P3.1 aria-label threads error state into the screen-reader announcement so it matches what sighted users see. Sub-finding: during hardening verification, Biome's useSemanticElements caught the theme picker's button[role=radio] composite-widget pattern; refactored to native fieldset+legend+input[type=radio] for a net a11y improvement (real form control + auto-grouping + better screen-reader behavior).

designDecisions.ironRuleClosure: Rocky's iron-rule directive against the disabled 'Soon' placeholder forced step (b) forward into the same atomic. The original step-(a)-only ship was correct in scope but cut a corner with the placeholder. The hardening atomic absorbs step (b) cleanly: the switcher now ships with a live CTA + working dialog instead of a UX dead-end. Future plan-doc revisions in M-F will rewrite the step-by-step retrospective in this light.
designDecisions.p11ActiveCompanyGuard: Widened App.tsx guard from `companyId !== null` → `companyId !== null && companies.some(c => c.id === companyId)`. Replaces the one-shot `window.teamx.companies.list()` direct preload-bridge call with the reactive useCompanies hook so the effect re-runs when the list mutates on bus events. Net architecture win: single source of truth for the companies list (the hook), reactive auto-select (no stale-id silent-failure window), no double-fetch (hook + direct call would have duplicated network).
designDecisions.p21ActionableErrorRetry: Error row converted from disabled 'in a moment' copy to actionable DropdownMenuItem with onSelect → refetch(). Uses e.preventDefault() to stop the menu from closing on the retry click (so the user sees the loading state surface inside the menu rather than re-opening it manually). RefreshCw icon for the affordance.
designDecisions.p31AriaLabelThreading: triggerAriaLabel computed const replaces the inline conditional aria-label expression. When isError, label reads 'Workspace switcher — workspaces failed to load'; otherwise the prior 'Workspace switcher — active: <name>' shape preserves. Screen reader now hears what sighted users see.
designDecisions.createDialogValidationMirror: Slug regex /^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]{0,62}$/ + name trim ≤120 chars hard-coded as constants in the dialog. Mirrors the M-C step b handler contract exactly so a server rejection is a genuine concurrency edge (duplicate slug under race) rather than a trivial typo the client could have caught. SLUG_REGEX + MAX_NAME_LENGTH constants are pinned in the test for drift detection.
designDecisions.noOnSuccessInvalidationOnCreateMutation: useCreateCompany has NO onSuccess invalidation — the global useCompanyEventSync hook listens for company.created on the bus and invalidates ['companies']. A local invalidation here would double-invalidate AND re-establish the per-hook pattern the global sync was designed to replace. Negative test asserts useQueryClient is NOT imported in the hook — catches a future refactor that accidentally re-adds it.
designDecisions.themePickerSemanticRefactor: Biome's useSemanticElements rule flagged the original <button type='button' role='radio'> theme picker as preferring native <input type='radio'>. Refactored to <fieldset><legend>+<label><input type='radio' className='sr-only' /></label></fieldset> — keeps the button-style visual via Tailwind on the label, gains real form-control semantics + auto-grouping by name attribute. Not a workaround — a net a11y improvement. The negative test asserts role='radio' and role='radiogroup' do NOT reappear.
designDecisions.duplicateSlugFieldRouting: The main-side handler re-wraps SQL UNIQUE violations as 'slug "X" is already in use'. The dialog's onError pattern-matches that string + routes the error to the slug field via setSlugError instead of the generic submit footer. Keeps the user's eye on the offending field. Other errors land in the footer row.
designDecisions.noToastInfra: Success path closes the dialog + flips companyId — no toast, matching the existing HireDialog convention. A repo-wide toast/notification primitive does not exist today; introducing one would be a cross-cutting scope creep beyond M-D step (b). The switcher's re-render to show the new active workspace is the visual confirmation.
designDecisions.fragmentSiblingDialogMount: CreateCompanyDialog mounts as a sibling of DropdownMenu inside a fragment, not nested inside the menu content. Radix's DropdownMenu focus-trap would conflict with the dialog's focus-trap on open if nested; sibling mount lets each Radix root manage its own focus independently.

The hardening commit demonstrates the iron-rule discipline: a P1 surfaced by post-ship QA gets fixed inline rather than deferred, mirroring the M-C step d hardening precedent.

notes: M-D is the first sprint to run entirely on M-C's restored backend surface. Every UI component consumes a verified-on-disk IPC channel + subscribes to the invariant #11 bus events M-C wired. Zero backend additions in M-D scope.

Restoration order WITHIN M-D: (a) WorkspaceSwitcher foundation [SHIPPED]. (b) CreateCompanyDialog + step-a audit hardening [SHIPPED via step (a+b) collapse]. Workspace-switcher E2E pre-flight [SHIPPED via 1d04e77]. Next: (c) CompanySettings + HireDialog manager-select. (d) Chat tab enable (independent — can interleave with c). (e) OrgChartView read-side (independent — can interleave). (f) Org-chart interactions (depends on e). (g) E2E spec + final verification gate.
Each step is its own atomic + ledger. Allowlist preserved at 3 entries throughout M-D — the remaining 3 are mcp.* naming-drift rows scheduled for M-F docs-only IPC table renames.
Do NOT start M-F (Documentation Truth-Up) before M-D ships. M-F rewrites CLAUDE.md status blocks in three-bucket format which requires M-D exit-KPI as an input.

The Company wire shape widening to include archive status is deferred to step (c) alongside the CompanySettings archived-companies UX. In the dev default single-company seed, this has zero visible effect at step (a).

The cross-hook contract iterator in event-sync-hooks.test.ts is split into two iteration lists per step (a) — per-company hooks [use-tickets / meetings / projects / goals / employees] for the [companyId, qc] mount-once pattern, and ALL hooks [+ use-companies] for the ground-zero-audit citation. A future global-scope hook (e.g., use-providers or use-settings-sync) joins use-companies on the citation iteration but not the companyId-guarded iteration.
